name: Severe burns and scalds
summary: Dry-heat burns or wet-heat scalds. If it's hot, cool it.
recognition: Burns on face, hands, joints or genitals
Any burn on a young child

callEmergency.text: Call 999 for severe burns or scalds, burns to the face/airway, or any burn on a young child.

steps: Cool the burn under cool RUNNING water for at least 20 minutes.
Once cooled, lay loose strips of plastic cling film over the wound to protect from germs.
Don't burst blisters. Don't apply creams or ointments.
Keep them warm overall (burns lose body heat) but the burn itself stays cool.

actions.audience: Minor burn (hand on cooker, hot drink spill)
steps: Run under a cold tap for at least 20 minutes.
Apply a burns dressing if you have one.
Seek medical advice if it blisters or covers a large area.

warnings.text: Don't apply butter, oil, toothpaste, or ice.
Cling film loosely — don't wrap it tight, swelling will follow.

source: UKA First Aid Essentials · Module 2 — "If it's hot, cool it"
